seoTitle: How to Split a Ticket in Freshdesk
metaDescription: Learn how to split a Freshdesk ticket into two separate tickets when a conversation covers more than one issue.
intro: Sometimes a single ticket ends up covering two unrelated issues, making it hard to track resolution. This guide shows how to split a ticket in Freshdesk into two separate ones.

steps.heading: Open the ticket to split
steps.body: Go to the ticket containing the conversation that covers multiple issues.

steps.heading: Find the split option
steps.body: Locate the split ticket action, typically available in the ticket's menu of options.

steps.heading: Select the content to move
steps.body: Choose which part of the conversation should move into the new, separate ticket.

steps.heading: Confirm the split
steps.body: Confirm the action, which creates a new ticket while keeping the original conversation intact for the remaining issue.

faq.question: Does splitting a ticket create a brand new ticket number?
faq.answer: Yes, the split-off portion becomes a new ticket with its own ticket number.

faq.question: Can I split a ticket into more than two parts?
faq.answer: You can typically split off one section at a time, repeating the process if there are multiple unrelated topics.

faq.question: Does the customer see both tickets?
faq.answer: Yes, the customer will generally see both the original and the newly split ticket in their history.

faq.question: Can I assign the split ticket to a different agent?
faq.answer: Yes, once split, the new ticket can be assigned independently to whichever agent should handle it.
